ground_truth.title: Finding my balance: An evolved and simplified task management system
ground_truth.main_content: In this ever-changing world, with shifting demands and responsibilities, I have experimented through multiple task management strategies. E.g: Zettelkasten (much more than task management, but still), Eisenhower matrix, Getting things done (GTD) method, Agile/Kanban, ABCDE method, Franklin planner method, etc. Finally, I've settled on a system that works for me (in most cases). It's most likely a mash-up of various methods recommended from books, blog posts, conversations with experienced folks, etc. I mostly deal with unit items of tasks which are neither too small, nor too large. "Steps" to complete one item are not included (Not Too Small). Projects or ideas that might span a long time (months to years) are excluded (Not Too Large). P-0 is a very short list of things I would do "immediately". P-1 is a slightly larger list that contains the items that I need to address "sooner than later". P-2 is a large list of various obligations and tasks. P-X is a short list of things that excite me. Every day come up with tasks in two categories (~5/10 minutes daily time spend): Do (tasks planned for the day) and Look forward to (items that excite me). The current tool used is Microsoft OneNote for its simplicity and cross-device sync. This model doesn't apply to my family life at all, but I find myself following it about 80% of the time in my work/career life.
with: In this ever-changing world, with shifting demands and responsibilities, I have experimented through multiple task management strategies.
Finally, I've settled on a system that works for me (in most cases).
P-0: A very short list of things I would do "immediately".
Every day come up with tasks in two categories (~5/10 minutes daily time spend): Do and Look forward to.
I hope sharing this provides a window into my thoughts and methods, but it's essential to remember that this is purely my experience.
